title: My Classroom: Objects Vocabulary Pack (K-2 ELL)
description: A comprehensive vocabulary pack designed for K-2 ELL newcomers to learn and practice common classroom objects in English and Spanish. Includes vocabulary cards, a mini-book, and three engaging worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A yellow pencil with a pink eraser on top.
An open book with colorful illustrations.
A small classroom chair, blue or green.
A rectangular classroom table with four legs.
A single red crayon, peeled slightly.
A stack of white blank papers.
A pair of child-safe scissors with rounded tips and blue handles.
A white glue stick.
A colorful child's backpack, slightly open.
A blue felt-tip marker with its cap on.
A pink rectangular rubber eraser.
A clear plastic ruler with inch and centimeter markings.
A large white board mounted on a classroom wall, with a few scribbles.
A classroom door, slightly ajar, showing a hallway.
A classroom window looking out to a sunny playground.
A round analog wall clock showing 9:00.
A single student desk with a cubby underneath.
A desktop computer with a monitor, keyboard, and mouse.
A wooden classroom shelf with a few books and toys.
A bright yellow plastic folder, closed.

worksheets.instructions_en: Draw a line from the English word to its matching picture.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Dibuja una línea de la palabra en inglés a su imagen correspondiente.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the words below. Then say the word.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras de abajo. Luego di la palabra.

items.image_prompt: A simple drawing of a pencil.
A simple drawing of a book.
A simple drawing of a chair.
A simple drawing of a table.
A simple drawing of a crayon.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the classroom objects. Say their names in English and Spanish.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea los objetos del salón de clases. Di sus nombres en inglés y español.

items.image_prompt: An outline drawing of child-safe scissors.
An outline drawing of a glue stick.
An outline drawing of a child's backpack.
An outline drawing of a marker.
An outline drawing of an eraser.

mini_book.title: My Classroom / Mi Salón de Clases

pages.text_en: This is my classroom. It is big!
pages.text_l2: Este es mi salón de clases. ¡Es grande!

pages.text_en: I see a pencil. I use my pencil to write.
pages.text_l2: Veo un lápiz. Uso mi lápiz para escribir.

pages.text_en: I see a book. I like to read books.
pages.text_l2: Veo un libro. Me gusta leer libros.

pages.text_en: I see a chair. I sit on my chair.
pages.text_l2: Veo una silla. Me siento en mi silla.

pages.text_en: I see a table. I work at my table.
pages.text_l2: Veo una mesa. Trabajo en mi mesa.

pages.text_en: I see crayons. I color with crayons.
My classroom has many things! What do you see?
pages.text_l2: ¡Mi salón de clases tiene muchas cosas! ¿Qué ves tú?

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and name at least 15 common classroom objects in English and Spanish. Students will use new vocabulary in simple sentences.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary using flashcards and realia. Use the mini-book for shared reading and repeated practice. Worksheets can be completed independently or in small groups. Encourage students to point to and name objects in the classroom.
materials_needed: Real classroom objects (pencil, book, chair, etc.)
Glue sticks for optional extension activities (e.g., cut and paste)
differentiation_tips: **Support:** Provide sentence frames (e.g., 'It's a ___.', 'I see a ___ .'), use TPR (Total Physical Response) by having students touch or point to objects, pair students with a stronger English speaker, use visuals extensively.

**Challenge:** Ask students to describe objects (e.g., 'What color is the pencil?'), use objects in a sentence, identify objects by their function (e.g., 'What do we use to cut paper?'), create their own mini-book page.

answers.2: Visual check: Glue stick colored appropriately.
